scenarios.name: Festival Season (Diwali)
scenarios.context:
Diwali festival is coming in 2 weeks - expect very high demand for sweets, ghee, oil, and decorative items like diyas and candles.

Weather: Pleasant post-monsoon weather, good for shopping and celebrations.

Local events: Multiple family gatherings and community celebrations planned. Neighbors are preparing for house cleaning and decoration.

Market trends: Prices of dry fruits and ghee are rising due to festival demand. Competition is offering discounts on bulk purchases.

Customer behavior: Families are buying in larger quantities than usual. Premium items like dry fruits and branded sweets are in high demand.

scenarios.name: Monsoon Season
scenarios.context:
Heavy monsoon rains for the past 2 weeks - people staying indoors more and buying comfort foods.

Weather: Continuous rainfall, high humidity, roads sometimes waterlogged. Customers prefer quick shopping trips.

Local events: Schools closed due to heavy rains. More families at home during the day.

Market trends: Increased demand for tea, biscuits, instant foods, and health items like ginger and honey. Umbrellas and raincoats selling well.

Customer behavior: People buying more packaged and instant foods. Preference for items that don't spoil quickly due to humidity.

scenarios.name: Regular Business Days
scenarios.context:
Normal business operations during regular season - steady daily sales patterns.

Weather: Pleasant spring weather, good for daily shopping routines.

Local events: Normal school and office schedules. Regular community activities.

Market trends: Stable prices for most items. Seasonal vegetables like tomatoes showing some price variation.

Customer behavior: Regular daily purchases of essentials. Customers buying fresh items more frequently. Steady demand for staples like rice, dal, and oil.

Competition: Normal competitive environment. Focus on maintaining good relationships with regular customers.
